question: The concept of 'world systems theory' (Wallerstein)
options: The global economy is divided into core, semi-periphery and periphery nations
Caribbean economies remain structurally dependent on plantation-era patterns
Both Western feminism's universalism and postcolonialism's gender blindness
The fundamental nature and structure of social reality
answer: The global economy is divided into core, semi-periphery and periphery nations

question: 'The plantation economy model' (Best and Levitt)
options: Caribbean economies remain structurally dependent on plantation-era patterns
Who is responsible for climate change and who bears its costs
The fundamental nature and structure of social reality
Capitalism has always been racially organised from its origins
answer: Caribbean economies remain structurally dependent on plantation-era patterns

question: The concept of 'racial capitalism' (Robinson)
options: Some states determine who may live and who must die as an exercise of power
Multiple worldviews and ways of knowing can coexist as equals
Capitalism has always been racially organised from its origins
Citizens' right to shape urban space according to their collective needs
answer: Capitalism has always been racially organised from its origins

question: 'Postcolonial feminism' challenges
options: Capitalism has always been racially organised from its origins
Recognition of diverse knowledge systems as valid alongside Western science
Both Western feminism's universalism and postcolonialism's gender blindness
The global economy is divided into core, semi-periphery and periphery nations
answer: Both Western feminism's universalism and postcolonialism's gender blindness
